title: Boro's Latte Lath completes Â£22.5m move to MLS
content:
Emmanuel Latte Lath's Â£22.5m move to Atlanta United is a record deal for both Middlesbrough and MLS

Middlesbrough striker Emmanuel Latte Lath has joined Atlanta United for Â£22.5m, a record fee received by Boro.

Latte Lath completed the transfer, for what is also the highest fee paid in Major League Soccer (MLS) history, before Monday's transfer deadline.

The Ivory Coast international scored 27 goals in 59 Championship appearances afterjoining Middlesbroughfrom Swiss club St Gallen in August 2023.

Latte Lath, 26, will team up with former Newcastle United winger Miguel Almiron, whojoined the Georgia outfitin an Â£8m deal.

Middlesbrough have already moved to replace Latte Lath bysigning former Manchester City and Leicester City striker Kelechi Iheanachoon loan from Sevilla until the end of the season, following the addition ofMorgan Whittaker from Plymouth Argyle.

The Latte Lath deal is believed to bring the club about Â£17.6m up front and Â£4.9m in add-ons.

He was not involved in Monday'shome defeat by Sunderlandwhich left Boro outside the Championship play-off places on goal difference.
